description: Deep rhetorical structure analysis of 13 TOEFL 2026 academic passages

compare_contrast.note: Used within passages but never as primary pattern

conclusion_summary.note: ETS passages NEVER have a dedicated conclusion paragraph

received_wisdom_then_revision.note: Most common opening: state prior assumption, then signal change

consequence_implication.note: Extends the argument, pushes topic forward

limitation_concession.note: Opens with 'Despite', acknowledges limitations + ongoing research

benefits_then_limitations.description: Define technology → Explain mechanism → List benefits → Acknowledge limitations + research

chronological_three_stages.description: Primitive era → Intermediate/flawed → Breakthrough with lasting impact

discovery_zooming_in.description: Big discovery → Physical mechanism → Biological mechanism → Complex ecology

keyConventions: Every paragraph starts with a functioning topic sentence (100%)
90%+ of non-opening paragraphs reference the previous paragraph in their topic sentence
ETS passages NEVER have a dedicated conclusion/summary paragraph
'Beyond' is the signature additive transition for paragraph openings
'Despite' is the signature concessive marker for limitation paragraphs
Evidence/example paragraphs always appear in middle position, never first or last
Compare-contrast is used within paragraphs, never as the primary passage structure
Passage length correlates with difficulty: easy ~185 words, medium ~285, hard ~315
